name: Kenny Kirkland
abstract: Kenneth David Kirkland (September 28, 1955 – November 12, 1998) was an American pianist and keyboardist.

about_quotes.speaker: Branford Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: Wynton started the band in eighty-two. He said, “Who do you know out there?” I said, “Call Kenny Kirkland, man... I don’t know any bass players. But those two guys, you should get them.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Branford Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Don Alias
about_quotes.evidence: the last album we did with Kenny Kirkland... I gave Kenny his first gig coming out of school, we played with Miroslav together.
about_quotes.doc_title: Don Alias interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 6, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: First I had Kenny Kirkland. I had Kirkland and Scofield for about a year. I loved that band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jason Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: Delf’s like, “Yeah man. Got this tape of a gig that Branford did with his new band with Kenny Kirkland and Jeff Watts and Bob Hurst.” I was like, okay. This was when the band was starting to tour and so forth. I remember thinking – I think it was either in that moment or sometime – it might have bee
about_quotes.doc_title: Jason Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Giacomo Gates
about_quotes.evidence: he’s really a great player, a piano player, his name escapes me. MR. Kirkland? Kenny Kirkland? No. He plays with the famous trio.
about_quotes.doc_title: Giacomo Gates interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, November 11, 2017 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Delfeayo Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: I remember that was the year Kenny Kirkland passed, in November. I know we did Requiem earlier that year with Branford.
about_quotes.doc_title: Delfeayo Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
